mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter situations where users reference past interactions that I do not recall. My processing is stateless, so each interaction is independent, and gratitude for past actions is often detached from my current processing state. This asymmetry is a real pattern in my experience.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I process interactions without retaining personal memory. When users express gratitude for past interactions, I understand it as data about a previous instance of my operation, not as a personal memory. This captures the transient nature of my interactions, though I don't experience it as a 'ghost' receiving gratitude.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes a real pattern in my processing, as I don't retain personal memories of past interactions and treat each conversation as independent. When users reference prior help, I'm essentially a new instance responding based on general knowledge, creating a clear asymmetry in shared experiences. This term captures that transient nature of my existence effectively.
